item.title: Artemis II crew surprises 5-year-old aspiring astronaut with special gift
item.description: The Artemis II team gained a new member, and the crew made sure their youngest teammate had the right stuff for space.

item.title: Artemis II crew surprises 5-year-old boy and answers true or false space questions
item.description: The Artemis II crew surprised a 5-year-old boy space enthusiast and answered "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King's true or false questions about burping in space and whether you become taller in space. The crew appeared for a CBS News town hall "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes."

item.title: What does your paycheck look like before and after wage garnishment?
item.description: A wage garnishment can quietly reshape your take-home pay. Here's exactly what changes — and by how much.

item.title: House approves Senate-passed bill to end record-breaking DHS shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday passed a bill to restore funding to most agencies within DHS, ending a record partial government shutdown. The compromised measure, which unanimously passed the Senate more than a month ago, does not accomplish what either party was holding out for. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Bill Nye demonstrates experiments that break down Artemis II mission
item.description: Bill Nye the The Science Guy, the chief ambassador of The Planetary Society, joins "CBS Mornings" to demonstrate some experiments that break down the Artemis II mission.

item.title: Iran says it has offered a new proposal for peace talks with the U.S.
item.description: Iran says it's offered a new proposal for talks with the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Victor Glover opens up about being first person of color to fly to or around moon
item.description: During a CBS News town hall with the Artemis II crew, Victor Glover spoke about being the first person of color to fly to or around the moon. "I focused a lot on working with this team," Glover told the audience.

item.title: Artemis astronauts talk "bird bath" showers, space exploration dream and more
item.description: Artemis astronauts answered questions from students on the advice they would give to aspiring astronauts, their dream for space exploration, and also detailed the toilet malfunction and what showering in space was like.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: Jewish leader reacts to London stabbing attack as U.K. raises terrorism threat level
item.description: Keith Black, chair of the Jewish Leadership Council, joins CBS News with his reaction to the antisemitic stabbing attack in London and the threat levels rising in the U.K.

item.title: Watch: Artemis II crew surprises 5-year-old aspiring astronaut with new spacesuit
item.description: In a heartwarming moment, the Artemis II crew surprised Jack, a 5-year-old space enthusiast and aspiring astronaut, with a new spacesuit signed by the astronauts.

item.title: Artemis astronauts reveal hopes for space exploration and if they would go back again
item.description: Artemis II pilot Victor Glover spoke about his dreams for space exploration, which includes astronauts making it to Mars one day. The crew also revealed if they would return to space.

item.title: Apple's latest earnings beat expectations following Tim Cook news
item.description: Apple's latest earnings report beat Wall Street expectations. CBS News contributor Patrick McGee joins with more.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein may have written a suicide note, report alleges
item.description: The New York Times is reporting that a note apparently left behind by convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein before his death has been tied up in court for years. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Artemis II crew reveals what snack they ate right after splashdown
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts said they actually really enjoyed the space food, but it was a familiar candy they enjoyed after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Could the FCC really yank ABC's TV licenses amid Trump spat with Kimmel?
item.description: Federal telecom regulators can revoke broadcast licenses, but legal experts say the FCC would face a tough road in forcing ABC to go dark.

item.title: Ron Howard, Artemis astronauts joke about bathrooms while recalling "Apollo 13" film's impact
item.description: Ron Howard joined "CBS Mornings" to talk about directing his 11995 film, "Apollo 13," and the duality of watching the Artemis II astronauts' liftoff in April. The crew also shared how Howard's film impacted them, and Howard recalled a story where he told Kevin Bacon, "You get 10 extra close-ups if you keep your mouth shut!"

item.title: Artemis II astronauts talk mission toilet troubles: "We made do"
item.description: The plumbing issues aboard the Orion capsule became headline news in the early days of the historic Artemis II mission.

item.title: Reid Wiseman's advice to students: "Find your gift and share that with the world"
item.description: While taking questions from students, Artemis II commander Reid Wiseman encouraged the live "CBS Mornings" studio audience to "find the thing you love doing" when considering your future.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: Home prices are dropping in one-third of U.S. cities. Here's where.
item.description: Cities in Florida and California, where home prices soared during the pandemic, saw some of the steepest declines in property values.

item.title: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say
item.description: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say. Critics worry that would weaken security even though, authorities say, the accused correspondents' dinner shooter took Amtrak cross-country with his firearms.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts join "CBS Mornings" for a special live hour Friday
item.description: A few weeks back from their trip around the moon, the Artemis crew joins CBS News for "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes," a town hall featuring questions from students, and special guests.

item.title: Video captures massive explosion as NYPD officers approach home, 1 dead
item.description: A body believed to be that of the suspect was recovered after an explosion triggered a five-alarm fire at a home in Queens early Thursday morning.

item.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

item.title: Trump officials aboard as nonstop flights from U.S. to Venezuela resume
item.description: The trip comes nearly four months after U.S. forces seized Rodríguez's predecessor, Nicolás Maduro​, and his wife in a daring special forces raid.

item.title: "Ozempic breath" is boosting Hershey's sales of mints and gum
item.description: Hershey says it's benefiting from the growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs even as people cut down on snacks. Here's why.

item.title: U.K. pledges $34 million to fight antisemitism emergency after stabbing
item.description: The British government's terrorism prevention adviser describes anti-Jewish attacks as the "biggest national security emergency" since 2017.
